title: GPTOPT Guided Check Explanations
purpose: Plain-language explanations for readiness checks shown in Guided Mode.

display.refreshRate.label: Display refresh rate
display.refreshRate.summary: Confirms Windows is actually running the monitor at the expected high refresh rate.
display.refreshRate.whyItMatters: Halo can only feel as responsive as the display path allows. A 240 Hz monitor running at 60 Hz or 120 Hz adds visible delay and makes controller aim feel heavier.
display.refreshRate.goodState: Primary gaming display is detected at the intended resolution and refresh rate, normally 3840x2160 at 240 Hz for the user's Omen OLED.
display.refreshRate.badState: Wrong monitor, wrong resolution, or refresh below the expected value.
display.refreshRate.safeAction: Set the correct refresh rate in Windows display settings or NVIDIA Control Panel.
display.refreshRate.undo: Revert the refresh rate from Windows display settings if the screen becomes unstable.

power.plan.label: Windows power plan
power.plan.summary: Checks whether Windows is using a performance-oriented power plan.
power.plan.whyItMatters: Aggressive power saving can slow CPU wake behavior and make frame delivery less consistent during gameplay.
power.plan.goodState: High Performance, Ultimate Performance, or a verified GPTOPT gaming plan is active.
power.plan.badState: Balanced or vendor eco plans are active during a competitive session.
power.plan.safeAction: Switch to a known gaming power plan before launching Halo.
power.plan.undo: Switch back to Balanced after gaming if lower idle power is preferred.

timer.resolution.label: Timer resolution
timer.resolution.summary: Checks whether the system timer is held at the expected low-latency interval during gaming.
timer.resolution.whyItMatters: A stable 0.5 ms timer can improve consistency for frame pacing tools, input polling, overlays, and capture utilities.
timer.resolution.goodState: Timer is 0.5 ms while the GPTOPT timer holder is running.
timer.resolution.badState: Timer is higher than expected or no holder is active when the gaming profile expects one.
timer.resolution.safeAction: Start the repo-owned GPTOPT timer holder for the session.
timer.resolution.undo: Stop the timer holder or reboot.

usb.selectiveSuspend.label: USB selective suspend
usb.selectiveSuspend.summary: Checks whether Windows can power down USB devices during use.
usb.selectiveSuspend.whyItMatters: Controller, headset, and receiver devices can feel inconsistent if Windows power management suspends or wakes the USB path mid-session.
usb.selectiveSuspend.goodState: USB selective suspend is disabled for the active gaming power plan.
usb.selectiveSuspend.badState: USB selective suspend is enabled while using wired controller or audio devices.
usb.selectiveSuspend.safeAction: Disable USB selective suspend for AC power in the active gaming plan.
usb.selectiveSuspend.undo: Re-enable it in advanced power settings.

graphics.hags.label: Hardware-accelerated GPU scheduling
graphics.hags.summary: Checks the Windows HAGS setting used by the graphics stack.
graphics.hags.whyItMatters: HAGS can affect scheduling behavior, latency, and frame pacing. On this user's current baseline, HAGS on is the expected state.
graphics.hags.goodState: HAGS is on for the current RTX 5080 baseline unless testing proves otherwise.
graphics.hags.badState: HAGS differs from the selected profile without being part of a controlled test.
graphics.hags.safeAction: Leave it alone unless running a planned A/B test.
graphics.hags.undo: Change the Windows graphics setting back and reboot.

graphics.mpo.label: Multiplane overlay
graphics.mpo.summary: Checks whether Windows MPO is disabled for the gaming baseline.
graphics.mpo.whyItMatters: MPO can interact badly with overlays, borderless fullscreen, HDR, VRR, and capture tools on some systems. This user's baseline expects MPO disabled.
graphics.mpo.goodState: MPO is disabled for the current GPTOPT Halo profile.
graphics.mpo.badState: MPO is enabled when the profile expects it disabled, especially with overlay/display symptoms.
graphics.mpo.safeAction: Only change MPO through a backed-up GPTOPT action, not manually during a session.
graphics.mpo.undo: Restore the prior registry value from GPTOPT backup or remove the override and reboot.

windows.gameMode.label: Game Mode
windows.gameMode.summary: Checks whether Windows Game Mode matches the gaming profile.
windows.gameMode.whyItMatters: Game Mode changes how Windows prioritizes game workloads and background activity. The current baseline expects it enabled.
windows.gameMode.goodState: Game Mode is enabled for the Halo profile.
windows.gameMode.badState: Game Mode is disabled unexpectedly or changed without testing.
windows.gameMode.safeAction: Enable Game Mode before testing Halo performance.
windows.gameMode.undo: Turn Game Mode off in Windows Gaming settings.

windows.gameDvr.label: Game DVR and background capture
windows.gameDvr.summary: Checks whether Windows background recording features are disabled.
windows.gameDvr.whyItMatters: Background capture can add overhead, storage writes, and inconsistent frame pacing. Dedicated tools like CapFrameX, OBS, or RTSS are preferred when intentionally capturing.
windows.gameDvr.badState: Background capture is enabled without a reason.
windows.gameDvr.safeAction: Disable background capture in Windows Gaming settings.
windows.gameDvr.undo: Re-enable it if Xbox Game Bar recording is needed.

dwm.badTweaks.label: DWM tweak leftovers
dwm.badTweaks.summary: Checks for old registry tweaks that try to disable or alter Desktop Window Manager behavior.
dwm.badTweaks.whyItMatters: Modern Windows games rely on DWM even in borderless fullscreen. Bad legacy tweaks can create stutter, broken login behavior, HDR issues, or weird input feel.
dwm.badTweaks.goodState: No known bad DWM tweak keys are present.
dwm.badTweaks.badState: Legacy DWM keys like disabled composition or fake frame-latency overrides are present.
dwm.badTweaks.safeAction: Remove only known bad values with a backup.
dwm.badTweaks.undo: Restore the exported registry backup or system restore point.

halo.json.label: Halo Infinite video config
halo.json.summary: Checks the Halo settings file against the selected profile without blindly changing it.
halo.json.whyItMatters: Halo's in-game settings control render scale, frame targets, HDR, and visual effects that directly affect latency, visibility, and GPU load.
halo.json.goodState: Steam Halo config uses the expected resolution, borderless mode, 100 percent scale, VSync off, HDR preference, and 960/960 min-target frame settings when selected.
halo.json.badState: Unexpected scaling, VSync, wrong resolution, wrong FPS targets, or visual effects enabled against the competitive baseline.
halo.json.safeAction: Preview exact config changes before applying. Never rewrite unrelated Halo JSON fields.
halo.json.undo: Restore the Halo config backup or let Halo regenerate the file.

rtss.haloProfile.label: RTSS Halo profile
rtss.haloProfile.summary: Checks whether RTSS is capping Halo correctly and detecting the game process.
rtss.haloProfile.whyItMatters: A clean RTSS cap can improve frame pacing and controller feel. Stacked or wrong caps can create delay or uneven frame delivery.
rtss.haloProfile.goodState: HaloInfinite.exe profile exists with FramerateLimit 240 and ApplicationDetectionLevel 2 for the current baseline.
rtss.haloProfile.badState: No Halo profile, wrong cap, disabled detection, stealth/custom D3D settings causing capture or overlay issues, or multiple stacked limiters.
rtss.haloProfile.safeAction: Use the GPTOPT RTSS baseline action with backup, then retest frame pacing.
rtss.haloProfile.undo: Restore the previous RTSS profile file from backup.

apps.steam.label: Steam state
apps.steam.summary: Checks whether Steam is available for the Steam Halo install.
apps.steam.whyItMatters: The user's Halo setup is Steam-first. Xbox/Gaming Services should not be touched unless login or launch actually requires it.
apps.steam.goodState: Steam is installed and available before launching Halo.
apps.steam.badState: Steam missing, not running when needed, or Halo path cannot be found.
apps.steam.safeAction: Open Steam and verify Halo's install path.

apps.flydigi.label: Flydigi controller stack
apps.flydigi.summary: Checks whether the Flydigi/Vader controller stack is available.
apps.flydigi.whyItMatters: The user's controller feel depends on Flydigi SpaceStation, GameControllerService, and app-side stick settings. Killing it can change input behavior.
apps.flydigi.goodState: Flydigi SpaceStation or GameControllerService is running and the controller device is present.
apps.flydigi.badState: Controller service missing, device absent, or virtual mouse/keyboard behavior interfering with gameplay.
apps.flydigi.safeAction: Verify SpaceStation and device state before changing controller settings.
apps.flydigi.undo: Restart SpaceStation or reconnect the controller.

apps.sonar.label: SteelSeries Sonar audio routing
apps.sonar.summary: Checks whether the Sonar audio path needed for the headset is available.
apps.sonar.whyItMatters: The user needs Sonar for headphones. Disabling or killing it can break audio routing even if it looks like background bloat.
apps.sonar.goodState: SteelSeries/Sonar process, service, or audio device is present when the headset route depends on it.
apps.sonar.badState: Sonar route missing when selected as the active output/input path.
apps.sonar.safeAction: Keep Sonar running if the headset depends on it.
apps.sonar.undo: Restart SteelSeries GG/Sonar or select a different Windows audio output.

reboot.status.label: Pending reboot
reboot.status.summary: Explains whether Windows truly needs a reboot or only has app cleanup queued.
reboot.status.whyItMatters: A real Windows Update or component-servicing reboot can affect driver and system state. A single pending file rename is usually just cleanup and should not block play.
reboot.status.goodState: No Windows Update or CBS/component servicing reboot is pending.
reboot.status.badState: Windows Update or component servicing reports a real reboot requirement.
reboot.status.safeAction: Treat PendingFileRenameOperations-only as Review. Treat CBS/Windows Update reboot as Action.
reboot.status.undo: Reboot clears legitimate pending operations; stale cleanup entries may need separate review.

background.load.label: Background load
background.load.summary: Shows whether non-game apps are consuming CPU, GPU, disk, or memory during the session.
background.load.whyItMatters: High background load can steal scheduling time and create inconsistent frame pacing or input feel.
background.load.goodState: No unexpected heavy apps during a Halo session. Firefox can be ignored when it is only being used for ChatGPT troubleshooting.
background.load.badState: Unexpected scanners, launchers, updaters, RGB suites, browsers, or recording tools consuming resources during the run.
background.load.safeAction: Close only unnecessary apps. Do not kill RTSS, Afterburner, Flydigi, Sonar, Steam, or capture tools needed for the session.
